rulings.Case Title: BETTY UTTERBACK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Campbell Soup Co.¿S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 26, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Campbell Soup Company¿s motion for summary judgment: is continued per CCP 437c(h) to May 12, 2011 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 220 to permit plaintiffs to take the deposition of defendant¿s PMK/COR. Supplemental opposition papers must be filed no later than May 8. Supplemental reply papers must be filed no later than May 10. Supplemental opposition and reply papers are limited to the deposition of defendant¿s PMK/COR. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ELAINE PERCY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 26, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Plaintiffs¿ motion for leave to file second amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. The court¿s previously issued tentative ruling stated that if any party wanted a hearing, that party must notify the court by emailing contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm on April 18, and if a hearing was requested, it would be on April 26. The court did not receive notice that any party requested a hearing. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ERNEST STONE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Alexandra Nassopoulos
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 26, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant The W.W. Henry Co.¿s motion for determination of good faith settlement is granted. W. W. Henry Company met its burden of making a sufficient showing of all the relevant Tech-Built factors and J. E. Higgins Lumber Company failed to satisfy its burden of showing that the settlement between plaintiff and W. W. Henry was ¿so far outside the ballpark¿ as not to be in good faith. = (220/HEK)
